description: Replace the process-cwd workspace model with explicit path threading and tauri-plugin-store-backed active workspace state. Remove env::current_dir, workspace.rs, and the --workspace CLI flag entirely. Add NoActiveWorkspace variant to IssueListError. Update all existing unit and e2e tests to work with the new mechanism.

comments.text:
BACKGROUND AND GOALS

Currently Beadsmith determines the Current Workspace from env::current_dir(), set once at launch. The --workspace CLI flag (workspace.rs) overrides the process cwd for e2e tests. The bw subprocess inherits the process cwd implicitly through ProcessRunner (runner.rs does not call .current_dir()). The RPC layer's current_workspace_path() function in rpc.rs calls env::current_dir() to include the workspace path in responses.

ADR-0006 decides to remove process cwd entirely and thread the workspace path explicitly through Command::new("bw").current_dir(workspace_path). The backend holds the active workspace in memory, persisted via tauri-plugin-store. This is the foundation — every subsequent bead depends on this architectural change.

This bead is a pure infrastructure change: the UI behavior stays the same (the sidebar still shows the workspace path), but the source of that path changes from process cwd to the store. The workspace management commands (list, add, validate, switch) come in bead 2. The empty state and picker UI come in beads 3 and 4.

WHAT TO BUILD

1. Add tauri-plugin-store dependency: Run 'pnpm tauri add store'. This adds the Rust crate and registers the plugin. Add store permissions to capabilities/default.json (the existing capability file has core:default and opener:default — add the store permissions following the same pattern).

2. Create a WorkspaceStore trait (in a new src-tauri/src/workspace/ module or similar): This is the persistence seam, mirroring the CommandRunner pattern. Production uses a TauriStore impl backed by tauri-plugin-store; tests inject a fake. For this bead, the trait only needs active workspace path operations:
   - get_active_workspace_path() -> Option<String>
   - set_active_workspace_path(path: String) -> ()
   The workspace list operations (get_workspaces, set_workspaces) and the WorkspaceEntry type come in bead 2 when add_workspace and list_workspaces are implemented.

3. Backend active workspace state: The backend holds the active workspace path in memory, initialized on app launch from the store. If the store has no active workspace path (first launch), the active workspace is None. This state must be accessible from the TauRPC resolvers. The current BeadsmithApiImpl struct is Clone+Default with no state — it needs to gain access to the workspace state, likely via Tauri managed state (app.manage(WorkspaceState::new(store, active_path)) in the setup hook, then accessed in resolvers via tauri::State). The exact mechanism depends on TauPC's support for injecting Tauri State into resolver methods.

4. Modify CommandRunner trait (runner.rs): The run() method gains a workspace_path parameter so ProcessRunner can set .current_dir(workspace_path) on the Command. The new signature: run(&self, program: &str, args: &[&str], workspace_path: &str) -> io::Result<CommandOutput>. ProcessRunner::run calls Command::new(program).args(args).current_dir(workspace_path).output(). The trait doc comment currently says 'Implementations inherit the process current working directory' — update this to reflect the explicit workspace_path parameter.

5. Modify adapter functions (adapter.rs): list_all_issues, list_ready_issues, list_blocked_issues each gain a workspace_path: &str parameter. load_issues_for_view passes it to runner.run(). The env::current_dir() call in load_issues_for_view (used for logging) is replaced with the workspace_path parameter. The function signatures change from list_all_issues(runner: &dyn CommandRunner) to list_all_issues(runner: &dyn CommandRunner, workspace_path: &str).

6. Modify RPC layer (rpc.rs): load_issue_explorer_data and list_issues read the active workspace from backend state. If no active workspace: return IssueListError with kind NoActiveWorkspace. If active workspace exists: pass it to the adapter functions. The current_workspace_path() function (which calls env::current_dir()) is removed — the workspace_path in responses comes from the active workspace state. The free functions list_issues_from_adapter and load_issue_explorer_data_from_adapter gain a workspace_path parameter (or read it from injected state).

7. Add NoActiveWorkspace variant: Add NoActiveWorkspace to IssueListErrorKind enum (rpc.rs). Add the mapping in the From<ListIssuesError> impl — but wait, NoActiveWorkspace is not a ListIssuesError variant (that's the adapter error enum). NoActiveWorkspace is an RPC-level concept: when there's no active workspace, the RPC layer returns it directly without calling the adapter. So add NoActiveWorkspace to IssueListErrorKind and construct IssueListError { kind: NoActiveWorkspace, message: 'No workspace is configured. Add a Beadwork project directory to start browsing issues.' } directly in the RPC layer when the active workspace is None.

8. Remove workspace.rs module: Delete src-tauri/src/workspace.rs entirely (workspace_arg, apply_workspace_override, and all tests). Remove 'mod workspace;' from lib.rs. Remove the apply_workspace_override(std::env::args()) call from the run() function in lib.rs.

9. Register tauri-plugin-store in lib.rs: Add .plugin(tauri_plugin_store::Builder::default().build()) to the Tauri builder chain. Initialize the workspace state in the .setup() hook: create the store, read the active workspace path, and manage the WorkspaceState.

10. Update capabilities/default.json: Add store permissions (e.g., store:default or the specific allow-list permissions for the store plugin).

11. Frontend: Handle NoActiveWorkspace in IssueExplorerLoadState. Add a noWorkspace status to the union type: { status: 'noWorkspace' }. In issue-loader.ts, map the NoActiveWorkspace error kind to this state (the existing failure handler catches all errors — check if the error kind is noActiveWorkspace and route to noWorkspace instead of failure). In App.tsx, workspaceTextFor handles the noWorkspace state (returns 'No workspace' or similar). The IssueExplorer component receives the noWorkspace state — for this bead, it can render a minimal 'No workspace configured' message. The full empty state UI with Add Workspace button comes in bead 3. The sidebar footer shows 'No workspace' when in noWorkspace state.

WHY

- Process cwd is process-global state that creates race conditions during runtime workspace switching (bead 4). Threading the path explicitly eliminates this.
- Removing process cwd is a one-way door (ADR-0006) — once done, the app depends on the store for workspace resolution. This is intentional: it forces the workspace management flow to be the only path.
- The store is needed because without process cwd, the app needs to remember which workspace was active across restarts.
- The WorkspaceStore trait follows the CommandRunner pattern: a seam that lets unit tests inject a fake without needing tauri-plugin-store or a real filesystem.
- NoActiveWorkspace is a distinct state from NotBeadworkWorkspace: NoActiveWorkspace means 'no workspace selected at all'; NotBeadworkWorkspace means 'a workspace is selected but it is not a Beadwork directory'. The frontend routes them differently (empty state vs error display).

CONSIDERATIONS

- Developer experience regression: After this change, 'pnpm tauri dev' no longer auto-detects the workspace from cwd. The developer must add the workspace through the UI once; it persists across restarts. This is the same flow a real user goes through. Terminal-launch auto-detection could return as a future convenience but is out of scope.
- E2e test infrastructure: The existing e2e tests pass --workspace as a launch arg (wdio.issue-list.conf.ts appArgs). Removing --workspace breaks them. The replacement: seed the tauri-plugin-store with the workspace path before the app launches. Approach: add a BEADSMITH_STORE_PATH env var that the TauriStore impl checks — if set, use that path for the store file instead of the default platform-specific path. The e2e fixture (run-scenario.ts) writes a JSON file to that path containing { activeWorkspacePath: '<fixture-path>' } before launching the app. This is a test-only override, similar to how --workspace was test-only infrastructure. The existing e2e specs' workspace path assertion (expect(workspacePath).toContain(path.basename(launchedWorkspace))) should still pass because the path in the store matches the fixture path.
- CommandRunner signature change is breaking: Every existing test that calls FakeRunner::run() needs updating to pass a workspace_path. In adapter.rs tests, the FakeRunner::run implementation needs to accept and ignore (or record) the workspace_path. In rpc.rs tests, the FakeRunner similarly needs updating. Consider what workspace_path to use in tests — a dummy path like '/test/workspace' is fine since the FakeRunner doesn't actually run bw.
- The adapter's eprintln logging in load_issues_for_view currently logs env::current_dir(). Update it to log the workspace_path parameter.
- The generated TauRPC bindings (src/rpc/bindings.ts) will automatically include the new NoActiveWorkspace error kind when the Rust types change. The bindings are auto-generated at build time. The existing test 'generates_typescript_bindings' in rpc.rs will need updating to assert the new kind appears.
- The TauriStore impl needs to handle the case where the store file doesn't exist yet (first launch). tauri-plugin-store handles this gracefully (returns None for missing keys).
- The store file path: tauri-plugin-store writes to {app_config_dir}/{store_name}.json. The store name is configured when creating the StoreBuilder. Use a descriptive name like 'beadsmith' or 'workspace'.

TESTING

- Adapter unit tests (cargo test in adapter.rs): Update all FakeRunner tests to pass a workspace_path parameter to run(). The FakeRunner::run implementation should accept workspace_path (and optionally record it for assertions). Add a test asserting the workspace_path is passed through to the runner. Existing test assertions (command args, output interpretation) remain the same.
- RPC unit tests (cargo test in rpc.rs): Update FakeRunner tests for the new run() signature. Add tests for NoActiveWorkspace: when the active workspace is None, load_issue_explorer_data returns NoActiveWorkspace without calling the adapter. Add tests for successful load: when the active workspace is set, the workspace_path is threaded to the adapter and the response includes it. The 'generates_typescript_bindings' test needs updating to assert noActiveWorkspace appears in the bindings.
- Frontend tests (vitest): issue-loader.test.ts — add a test mapping NoActiveWorkspace to the noWorkspace state. App.test.tsx — add a test mocking loadIssueExplorerStateFromTauRpc to return noWorkspace state and asserting the minimal 'No workspace configured' rendering.
- E2e tests (WebDriver): Update wdio.issue-list.conf.ts to remove --workspace from appArgs. Update run-scenario.ts to seed the store file (write JSON to BEADSMITH_STORE_PATH) before launching the app. The existing issue-list.success.spec.ts and issue-list.empty.spec.ts should pass with the same assertions. The workspace path assertion in success.spec.ts should still work because the store contains the fixture path.
- workspace.rs tests: Deleted along with the module.

title: Foundation: thread workspace path, add store, remove process cwd
